match_story.headline: Pipeline test match
match_story.summary: Test run with 54 rallies across 2 games.

shot_choice_issues.description: Passive lifts/clears when net play available

short.tier_summary: Short rallies (≤5 shots): 11 won, 8 lost out of 19. Biggest differentiator: rally ending.

winning_reasons.narrative: Strong attacking initiative: attacking 0% vs 0% in lost rallies

medium.tier_summary: Medium rallies (6-12 shots): 13 won, 11 lost out of 24. Biggest differentiator: rally ending.

winning_reasons.narrative: Strong shot quality: avg effectiveness 83 vs 48 in lost rallies

long.tier_summary: Long rallies (13+ shots): 4 won, 7 lost out of 11. Biggest differentiator: rally ending.

rally_blueprints.description: Opponent reset shots dominant approach in short rallies: reset_shots (60%), serve_shots (20%), attacking_shots (10%)
Opponent reset shots dominant approach in medium rallies: reset_shots (47%), defensive_shots (18%), placement_shots (12%)

noteworthy_findings.observation: Effectiveness peaks in Endgame (100.0) and drops in Start (52.1)
noteworthy_findings.why: A 48 point effectiveness gap between scoring phases suggests inconsistency under different game situations.

noteworthy_findings.observation: Championship phase effectiveness rises by 24 points vs overall
noteworthy_findings.why: Player elevates performance when it matters most.

noteworthy_findings.observation: Plays better when leading (69.2) than when trailing (50.6)
noteworthy_findings.why: Strong front-runner — builds on lead effectively. May struggle to come from behind.
